terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.section1Content: By accessing and using Philosify, you accept and agree to be bound by these Terms of Service.
terms.section2Content: You must create an account to use paid features. Keep your credentials secure.
terms.section3Content: Prices displayed in USD. Payments processed via Stripe. All sales final unless otherwise stated.
terms.section4Content: New users receive 2 free analyses. Additional analyses: $0.60 each or via credit packages.
terms.section5Content: You agree not to misuse the Service, including attempting to access unauthorized areas or disrupt the system.
terms.section6Content: All content and analysis generated by Philosify remains the property of Philosify.
terms.section7Content: The Service is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Philosify values your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your information.
privacy.section1Content: We collect: Personal Information (name, email, payment via Stripe), Usage Data (browser, device, pages visited).
privacy.section2Content: To provide and improve our Service, process transactions via Stripe, and communicate updates.
privacy.section3Content: All payments handled by Stripe. We don't store full credit card information.
privacy.section4Content: We use cookies to enhance experience. Control via browser settings.
privacy.section5Content: We implement security measures to protect your data, but no method is 100% secure.
privacy.section6Content: We use third-party services (Supabase, Stripe, OpenAI, Google, Anthropic) which have their own privacy policies.
privacy.section7Content: You can request access, correction, or deletion of your personal data.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.content:
1. Acceptance of Terms

By accessing and using Philosify, you accept and agree to be bound by the terms and provision of this agreement.

2. Use License

Permission is granted to use Philosify for personal, non-commercial use. This license shall automatically terminate if you violate any of these restrictions.

3. Service Description

Philosify provides philosophical analysis of music using AI technology. The analysis is based on Objectivist philosophy principles.

4. Credits and Payments

Users receive free credits upon signup. Additional credits can be purchased through our payment system. All sales are final.

5. User Conduct

You agree not to use the service for any unlawful purpose or to violate any laws in your jurisdiction.

6. Intellectual Property

The service and its original content, features, and functionality are owned by Philosify and are protected by international copyright laws.

7. Disclaimer

The service is provided "as is" without any warranties, expressed or implied. We do not guarantee the accuracy or completeness of any content.

8. Limitation of Liability

Philosify shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential or punitive damages resulting from your use of the service.

9. Changes to Terms

We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Continued use of the service after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.

10. Contact

For questions about these Terms, please contact us at bob@philosify.org

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.content:
1. Information We Collect

We collect information you provide directly to us, including your email address, name, and payment information when you create an account or make purchases.

2. How We Use Your Information

We use your information to:

  • Provide, maintain, and improve our services
  • Process transactions and send related information
  • Send technical notices and support messages
  • Respond to your comments and questions

3. Information Sharing

We do not sell, trade, or rent your personal information to third parties. We may share your information with service providers who assist us in operating our service.

4. Data Storage and Security

We use Supabase for authentication and data storage. Your data is encrypted and stored securely. We implement industry-standard security measures to protect your information.

5. Cookies and Tracking

We use cookies and similar tracking technologies to track activity on our service and hold certain information to improve your experience.

6. Third-Party Services

We use third-party services including:

  • Stripe for payment processing
  • Supabase for authentication and database
  • Cloudflare for hosting and content delivery
  • OpenAI, Google, and other AI providers for analysis

7. Your Rights

You have the right to access, update, or delete your personal information. You can do this through your account settings or by contacting us.

8. Children's Privacy

Our service is not intended for children under 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13.

9. Changes to This Policy

We may update this privacy policy from time to time. We will notify you of any changes by posting the new policy on this page.

10. Contact Us

If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at bob@philosify.org

constellation.searchHint: Start typing to search 2,600 years of philosophical thought
constellation.webglHint: Your browser may not support WebGL

descriptions.mahavira: Ahimsa (non-violence); anekantavada (many-sidedness of truth); liberation through asceticism
descriptions.buddha: Four Noble Truths; Eightfold Path; impermanence and non-self (anatta)
descriptions.nagarjuna: Emptiness (sunyata); two truths doctrine; middle way between eternalism and nihilism
descriptions.shankara: Brahman alone is real; world is illusion (maya); Atman is Brahman; liberation through knowledge
descriptions.ramanuja: Qualified non-dualism; souls as real parts of Brahman; bhakti (devotion) path to liberation
descriptions.laozi: The Tao that can be told is not the eternal Tao; wu wei (non-action); simplicity and naturalness
descriptions.confucius: Ren (benevolence); li (ritual propriety); the junzi (noble person) cultivates virtue
descriptions.mozi: Universal love without partiality; consequentialist ethics; opposition to offensive warfare
descriptions.mencius: Human nature is inherently good; benevolent government; four beginnings of virtue
descriptions.zhuangzi: Butterfly dream (what is reality?); embrace spontaneity; all perspectives are relative
descriptions.xunzi: Human nature is evil; goodness is acquired; ritual and education transform nature
descriptions.han_fei: Law and punishment maintain order; the ruler must be impersonal; human nature is self-interested
descriptions.thales: First philosopher; rational principles in nature
descriptions.anaximander: Apeiron as first principle; rational cosmology
descriptions.heraclitus: Flux over permanence; paradox over logic; logos invoked but reason never systematized — seeds of irrationalism
descriptions.parmenides: Being is one; logical monism over the senses
descriptions.empedocles: Four elements; natural causation without myth
descriptions.democritus: Atomism; materialist reason; void and atoms
descriptions.protagoras: Man is the measure; cognitive relativism
descriptions.thrasymachus: Justice is the advantage of the stronger
descriptions.socrates: Dialectic method; logos; the examined life
descriptions.plato: Eternal Forms; objective truth; reason over senses
descriptions.aristotle: Logic; empiricism; objective science and ethics
descriptions.lucretius: De Rerum Natura; atomism; reason over religion
descriptions.chrysippus: Systematic Stoic logic; master of the syllogism
descriptions.cicero: Natural law; rational statecraft and rhetoric
descriptions.epictetus: Logos; only what is in our rational control
descriptions.plotinus: The One beyond reason; mystical emanation; reality is graded emanation from ineffable source
descriptions.porphyry: Neoplatonist mystic; reason as ladder to supra-rational unity
descriptions.augustine: Faith over reason; original sin; City of God; divine illumination
descriptions.proclus: Systematic Neoplatonism; divine intellect above discursive reason
descriptions.al_kindi: First Arab philosopher; reason and revelation compatible
descriptions.al_ghazali: Incoherence of philosophers; faith over reason
descriptions.anselm: Faith precedes reason; ontological argument subordinates logic to theology — reason as servant of revelation
descriptions.maimonides: Rationalist theology; reason compatible with Torah
descriptions.thomas_aquinas: Reason and faith reconciled; natural law
descriptions.bernard_of_clairvaux: Love and faith over dialectical reason
descriptions.meister_eckhart: Mystical union with God beyond rational concepts
descriptions.erasmus: Christian humanism; rational reform of the Church
descriptions.machiavelli: Political reason divorced from religious morality
descriptions.giordano_bruno: Infinite universe; reason and science over dogma
descriptions.la_boetie: Discourse on Voluntary Servitude; tyranny rests on consent; liberty through refusal
descriptions.montaigne: Que sais-je? — radical self-doubt suspends all objective knowledge; skepticism as a way of life
descriptions.francis_bacon: Inductive method; idols of the mind; new science
descriptions.john_locke: Tabula rasa; experience as the ground of knowledge
descriptions.george_berkeley: Esse est percipi — existence requires a perceiver; denies mind-independent reality
descriptions.rene_descartes: Cogito; clear and distinct ideas; method of doubt
descriptions.baruch_spinoza: Geometric ethics; reason as the highest freedom
descriptions.malebranche: Vision in God; occasionalism; Cartesian theology
descriptions.leibniz: Principle of sufficient reason; monads; calculus
descriptions.hugo_grotius: Natural law foundation; law of nations; rights independent of theology
descriptions.john_milton: Areopagitica; freedom of thought and press as absolute; individual conscience
descriptions.algernon_sidney: Discourses Concerning Government; republican liberty; pre-Locke rights theory
descriptions.isaac_newton: Mathematical principles of natural philosophy; universal gravitation; scientific method
descriptions.blaise_pascal: Heart has reasons reason cannot know; the wager
descriptions.giambattista_vico: Verum factum: we know only what we make — denies objective reality independent of human construction
descriptions.montesquieu: Rational analysis of laws; separation of powers
descriptions.voltaire: Reason vs. superstition, tyranny, and fanaticism
descriptions.adam_smith: Rational self-interest; invisible hand; moral sentiments
descriptions.lessing: Rational theology; toleration; education of humanity
descriptions.condorcet: Indefinite human progress through reason and science
descriptions.john_adams: Mixed government; balance of powers; republican virtue
descriptions.thomas_paine: Natural rights; popular sovereignty; anti-monarchy; "Common Sense"
descriptions.thomas_jefferson: Natural rights; "life, liberty, pursuit of happiness"; separation of church and state
descriptions.james_madison: Federalism; checks and balances; faction theory; Bill of Rights
descriptions.mary_wollstonecraft: A Vindication of the Rights of Woman; reason has no sex; equality through education
descriptions.wilhelm_von_humboldt: The Limits of State Action; individual development as the highest end; minimal government
descriptions.david_hume: Causation is habit not necessity; induction unjustifiable — destroys the rational foundations of science
descriptions.rousseau: Nature and sentiment over civilization and reason
descriptions.edmund_burke: Tradition and prejudice over abstract rationalism
descriptions.hamann: Faith and language over Enlightenment reason
descriptions.jacobi: Leap of faith; Glaubensphilosophie against Spinoza
descriptions.herder: Cultural particularity; Volksgeist over universal reason
descriptions.immanuel_kant: Thing-in-itself unknowable; reason bounded; duty-ethics divorced from reality — arch-enemy of Objectivism
descriptions.jeremy_bentham: Felicific calculus: aggregate utility can override individual rights — collectivism in rational disguise
descriptions.de_maistre: Theocratic authority and tradition over rationalism
descriptions.fichte: Absolute Ego as primary; collapses objective reality into subject
descriptions.hegel: Dialectical Spirit; individual dissolved into collective History; root of totalitarianism
descriptions.schelling: Nature philosophy; subject-object identity; late mysticism
descriptions.auguste_comte: Three stages; science replaces theology and metaphysics
descriptions.frederic_bastiat: The Law; property rights; the state as legal plunder; broken window fallacy
descriptions.alexis_de_tocqueville: Democracy in America; liberty over equality; tyranny of the majority; civic virtue
descriptions.frederick_douglass: Natural rights to demolish slavery; reason as liberation; self-ownership
descriptions.lord_acton: Power corrupts absolutely; liberty as the supreme political value; conscience over authority
descriptions.ralph_waldo_emerson: Over-Soul; intuition over reason; nature mysticism; self-reliance
descriptions.henry_david_thoreau: Civil Disobedience; mystical individualism; simplicity; conscience over law
descriptions.max_stirner: The Ego and Its Own; all abstractions are spooks; pure egoism; the unique one
descriptions.lysander_spooner: No Treason; natural rights; Constitution has no authority; individual over state
descriptions.auberon_herbert: Voluntary taxation; no coercion ever justified; individual sovereignty; the true liberty
descriptions.pierre_joseph_proudhon: Property is theft; mutualist anarchism; federalism; anti-state, anti-capitalist
descriptions.mikhail_bakunin: Collectivist anarchism; destroy the state and God; revolutionary violence
descriptions.friedrich_engels: Co-author of Communist Manifesto; historical materialism; dialectics of nature
descriptions.peter_kropotkin: Mutual aid; communist anarchism; no individual property; scientific anarchism
descriptions.schopenhauer: Blind Will underlies reality; reason is its servant
descriptions.nietzsche: Will to Power; death of God; perspectivism
descriptions.john_stuart_mill: Harm principle subordinates individual to society; utility trumps rights — altruism with a logical veneer
descriptions.kierkegaard: Leap of faith; subjectivity is truth; against Hegel
descriptions.karl_marx: Dialectical materialism inverts Hegel but retains irrationalism; collectivism; anti-individual
descriptions.charles_peirce: Truth as long-run consensus of inquirers — replaces correspondence to reality with social agreement
descriptions.william_james: Truth is what works — explicitly rejects correspondence theory of truth; pragmatic cash-value
descriptions.john_dewey: Instrumentalism; truth as tool for biological adaptation; no objective standards independent of use
descriptions.gottlob_frege: Logical foundations of mathematics; objectivity of sense
descriptions.edmund_husserl: Phenomenological reduction brackets objective reality; consciousness constitutes the world
descriptions.henri_bergson: Élan vital; intuition over intellect and mechanism
descriptions.max_weber: Disenchantment: reason strips meaning from the world; value-free science cannot ground ethics — fact-value split
descriptions.alfred_whitehead: Process philosophy; reality as becoming not being; organism over mechanism
descriptions.emma_goldman: Anarcho-communism; free love; anti-capitalism; individual freedom through collective liberation
descriptions.albert_jay_nock: Our Enemy the State; radical individualism; the remnant; anti-mass society
descriptions.h_l_mencken: The iconoclast of individualism; enemy of mass conformity; critic of democracy
descriptions.isabel_paterson: The God of the Machine; founding mother of American libertarianism; energy circuit of production
descriptions.rose_wilder_lane: The Discovery of Freedom; individual sovereignty; founding mother of libertarianism
descriptions.carl_menger: Founder of Austrian School; subjective theory of value; Principles of Economics (1871); methodological individualism
descriptions.eugen_bohm_bawerk: Capital and Interest; critique of Marx exploitation theory; time preference; roundabout production
descriptions.ludwig_von_mises: Human Action; praxeology; rational economic calculation; critique of socialism
descriptions.henry_hazlitt: Economics in One Lesson; seen and unseen; close friend and ally of Ayn Rand
descriptions.friedrich_hayek: Spontaneous order; Road to Serfdom; knowledge problem; critique of central planning
descriptions.milton_friedman: Capitalism and Freedom; monetarism; empiricist methodology; free markets; school choice
descriptions.george_stigler: Regulatory capture theory; economics of information; Nobel 1982; critique of government intervention
descriptions.gary_becker: Human capital theory; economics of discrimination; Nobel 1992; rational choice applied to all behavior
descriptions.thomas_sowell: Knowledge and Decisions; Basic Economics; critique of egalitarianism; constrained vs unconstrained visions
descriptions.karl_jaspers: Existenz; transcendence; limits of reason; boundary situations
descriptions.g_e_moore: Common sense realism; naturalistic fallacy in ethics
descriptions.ludwig_wittgenstein: Late: language games dissolve objective meaning; meaning is use, not correspondence to reality
descriptions.bernard_williams: Integrity and internal reasons dissolve objective moral standards — moral relativism with analytic credentials
descriptions.john_searle: Chinese Room argument; social construction of reality; intentionality
descriptions.saul_kripke: Naming and Necessity; rigid designators; modal logic; necessity a posteriori
descriptions.a_j_ayer: Language Truth and Logic; strict verificationism
descriptions.martin_heidegger: Being over cognition; Dasein; critique of Cartesian reason
descriptions.jean_paul_sartre: Existence precedes essence — denies human nature; radical freedom has no rational grounding in reality
descriptions.simone_de_beauvoir: Existentialist feminism; situation over nature; no objective human essence or sex-based identity
descriptions.albert_camus: Absurdism: universe is irrational and indifferent; revolt without rational foundation
descriptions.merleau_ponty: Embodied cognition dissolves subject-object distinction; no mind-independent reality accessible
descriptions.paul_ricoeur: Hermeneutics; all understanding is interpretation; no unmediated access to objective meaning
descriptions.walter_benjamin: Marxist aesthetics; dialectical image; history as catastrophe — no rational progress
descriptions.max_horkheimer: Instrumental reason as domination; Enlightenment itself becomes myth — attacks reason at its root
descriptions.herbert_marcuse: One-dimensional man; technological reason as repression
descriptions.theodor_adorno: Negative dialectics; no positive knowledge possible; Enlightenment inevitably turns into barbarism
descriptions.jurgen_habermas: Communicative reason is intersubjective not objective — truth by consensus, not by correspondence to reality
descriptions.noam_chomsky: Innate grammar; generative rationalism; language universals
descriptions.thomas_kuhn: Paradigm shifts; scientific revolutions incommensurable — no objective progress in science
descriptions.barbara_branden: The Passion of Ayn Rand; early movement organizer
descriptions.nathaniel_branden: Psychology of self-esteem; chief popularizer of Objectivism
descriptions.hannah_arendt: Political action over individual reason; vita activa over vita contemplativa — the collective precedes the individual
descriptions.louis_althusser: Scientific Marxism; individuals are effects of ideology — no autonomous rational subject
descriptions.john_rawls: Veil of ignorance erases individual identity; distributive justice violates rights; egalitarianism over merit
descriptions.robert_nozick: Libertarian natural rights; self-ownership; Anarchy State and Utopia — closest liberal to Objectivism
descriptions.lyotard: Incredulity toward metanarratives; end of grand reason
descriptions.jacques_derrida: Deconstruction; all texts endlessly defer meaning
descriptions.alasdair_macintyre: Tradition-dependent rationality — no universal reason, only internal standards of practices
descriptions.richard_rorty: No mirror of nature; truth is contingent and social
descriptions.murray_rothbard: Anarcho-capitalism; utopian stateless society; competing defense agencies; anti-IP; subjectivist ethics; no objective law
descriptions.hilary_putnam: No view from nowhere; internal realism denies mind-independent objective reality — Kant recycled
descriptions.alvin_plantinga: Belief in God as properly basic — bypasses reason entirely; faith without rational justification is anti-reason
descriptions.david_lewis: Modal realism; possible worlds; logical rigor
descriptions.derek_parfit: Personal identity; objective reasons; reasons and persons
descriptions.nick_bostrom: Simulation argument; existential risk; rational futurism
descriptions.edwin_locke: The Prime Movers (2000); goal-setting theory; Objectivist psychology of achievement
descriptions.allan_gotthelf: Gotthelf on Aristotle; philosophy of biology; Objectivist scholar
descriptions.harry_binswanger: How We Know (2014); consciousness; ARI senior fellow
descriptions.andrew_bernstein: The Capitalist Manifesto; Objectivism in history and culture
descriptions.peter_schwartz: In Defense of Selfishness (2015); The Foreign Policy of Self-Interest; ARI senior fellow
descriptions.yaron_brook: Equal Is Unfair; ARI executive chairman; Objectivist activism
descriptions.robert_mayhew: s Q&A volumes; Essays on Ayn Rand
descriptions.darryl_wright: Objectivist ethics and metaethics; Harvey Mudd College; normative theory
descriptions.craig_biddle: Loving Life (2002); The Objective Standard; Objectivist ethics
descriptions.gregory_salmieri: A Companion to Ayn Rand (2016); Objectivist epistemology
descriptions.elan_journo: Winning the Unwinnable War (2009); ARI fellow; foreign policy and self-interest
descriptions.don_watkins: Equal Is Unfair (2016); Free Market Revolution; ARI fellow; capitalism and rights
descriptions.david_kelley: The Evidence of the Senses; open Objectivism; Atlas Society
descriptions.robert_tracinski: The Tracinski Letter; Rand-influenced cultural and political commentary
descriptions.walter_block: Rothbardian anarcho-capitalism; utopian; anti-IP; NAP without objective law; subjectivist framework
descriptions.david_friedman: Consequentialist anarcho-capitalism; utopian; competing private defense agencies; utilitarian ethics; anti-IP
descriptions.hans_hermann_hoppe: Kantian argumentation ethics (transcendental deduction); utopian natural order; anti-democracy and anti-objective-law; praxeology; anti-IP
descriptions.peter_singer: Impartial utilitarianism; rational but demands self-sacrifice; animal rights dissolve human exceptionalism
descriptions.martha_nussbaum: Capabilities require redistribution overriding individual rights; cosmopolitan obligations deny national sovereignty
descriptions.sam_harris: Moral landscape; science can determine values; critique of religion and faith
descriptions.christopher_hitchens: Antitheism; religion poisons everything; Enlightenment values; free expression
descriptions.richard_dawkins: Selfish gene; memes; evolution as explanation; militant atheism; God delusion
descriptions.slavoj_zizek: Lacanian Marxism; the Real is inaccessible; ideology pervades all apparent rationality
descriptions.averroes: Aristotle's great commentator; reason is autonomous
descriptions.duns_scotus: Will primacy over intellect; divine voluntarism — God's will not bound by reason
descriptions.schiller: Aesthetic education; play-drive dissolves rational-natural divide; Kant's heir in aesthetics
descriptions.emmanuel_levinas: Ethics as first philosophy before metaphysics — inverts Rand's hierarchy; the Other commands absolutely
descriptions.ayn_rand: Reason as man's absolute; Objectivism; rational self-interest
descriptions.leonard_peikoff: Rand's designated heir; OPAR (1991); founder of ARI
descriptions.michael_berliner: ARI co-founder and former executive director; Rand's philosophy and education reform
descriptions.onkar_ghate: ARI chief philosophy officer; Rand's epistemology and ethics
descriptions.ben_bayer: ARI fellow; epistemology; Rand's theory of concepts and perception
descriptions.albert_einstein: Relativity; E=mc²; unified field theory; determinism vs. quantum mechanics; pacifism
descriptions.aleksandr_solzhenitsyn: Gulag Archipelago; critique of Soviet terror; individual conscience; moral recovery
descriptions.alexander_hamilton: Strong federal government; national bank; manufacturing economy; constitutional interpretation
descriptions.antonio_gramsci: Cultural hegemony; organic intellectuals; war of position vs. war of maneuver; Prison Notebooks
descriptions.basava: Anti-caste movement; devotional equality; rejection of ritual; social justice
descriptions.benedetto_croce: Absolute historicism; aesthetics as intuition; philosophy as methodology of history
descriptions.benjamin_franklin: Self-improvement; practical virtue; scientific inquiry; American independence
descriptions.carl_jung: Collective unconscious; archetypes; individuation; synchronicity; psychological types
descriptions.chanakya: Arthashastra; realpolitik; statecraft; ends justify means in governance
descriptions.daniel_kahneman: Thinking Fast and Slow; cognitive biases; prospect theory; bounded rationality
descriptions.diogenes_of_sinope: Rejection of material possessions; live according to nature; virtue through ascetic practice
descriptions.edith_stein: Empathy as philosophical concept; phenomenology of the person; synthesis with Thomism
descriptions.elizabeth_anscombe: Modern moral philosophy critique; intention; action theory; revival of virtue ethics
descriptions.frederic_passy: Peace through free trade; international arbitration; first Nobel Peace Prize
descriptions.galileo_galilei: Heliocentrism; experimental method; mathematics as language of nature; telescopic observations
descriptions.george_orwell: Critique of totalitarianism; Newspeak; doublethink; language and thought; Animal Farm; 1984
descriptions.harriet_taylor_mill: Women's rights; co-author with J.S. Mill; marriage equality; enfranchisement
descriptions.hildegard_of_bingen: Divine visions; holistic medicine; sacred music; cosmic harmony
descriptions.hypatia: Mathematics and astronomy; rational inquiry; preservation of classical knowledge
descriptions.isaiah_berlin: Negative vs. positive liberty; value pluralism; hedgehog vs. fox; history of ideas
descriptions.jacques_lacan: The unconscious is structured like a language; mirror stage; the Real, Symbolic, Imaginary
descriptions.jose_ortega_y_gasset: Revolt of the masses; perspectivism; I am I and my circumstances; vital reason
descriptions.leonardo_da_vinci: Observation as source of knowledge; art and science unified; anatomy; engineering
descriptions.maria_montessori: Child-centered education; prepared environment; auto-education; sensitive periods
descriptions.philippa_foot: Trolley problem; natural goodness; virtue ethics revival; moral realism
descriptions.plutarch: Parallel Lives; moral biography; virtue through example; character shapes destiny
descriptions.ram_mohan_roy: Abolition of sati; monotheism; education reform; synthesis of East and West
descriptions.rosa_luxemburg: Spontaneous revolution; critique of nationalism; democracy within socialism; anti-imperialism
descriptions.sigmund_freud: Unconscious mind; id-ego-superego; repression; Oedipus complex; dream interpretation
descriptions.simone_weil: Attention as spiritual practice; gravity and grace; decreation; worker solidarity
descriptions.sri_aurobindo: Spiritual evolution; supramental consciousness; integral yoga; divine life on earth
descriptions.steven_pinker: Language instinct; evolutionary psychology; Enlightenment Now; decline of violence; rational optimism
descriptions.thucydides: History as rigorous inquiry; power politics; Melian dialogue on might vs. right
descriptions.viktor_frankl: Man's Search for Meaning; will to meaning; logotherapy; freedom to choose attitude
descriptions.wang_yangming: Unity of knowledge and action; innate moral knowledge; heart-mind as source of truth
descriptions.winston_churchill: Democracy as worst system except all others; resistance to tyranny; individual liberty; Western civilization
descriptions.zhu_xi: Li (principle) and qi (vital force); investigation of things; moral cultivation

underground.title: The Underground
underground.description: A single global room for contrarian takes, challenging mainstream scores, and deep philosophical debate. Longer messages (1000 chars). Once unlocked, access is permanent.
underground.chooseIdentityDesc: Pick a nickname for The Underground. This will be shown on your posts instead of your real name.
underground.noConfessions: No confessions yet. Be the first to share your thoughts.
underground.deleteConfirm: Delete this post? This cannot be undone.

debate.title: Debates
debate.description: Engage in structured philosophical debates. Pose questions, present arguments, and get an AI-powered Philosify wrap-up with a philosophical verdict.
debate.questionPlaceholder: What philosophical question should we debate?
debate.contextPlaceholder: Add context or your opening argument (optional)
debate.noDebates: No debates yet. Start the first one!
debate.noReplies: No arguments yet. Be the first to weigh in!
debate.wrapupDescription: Get an AI-powered philosophical summary and verdict for this debate.
debate.inviteHint: Share this link to invite someone to this debate
debate.inviteRateLimit: Too many invitations. Please wait a moment.
debate.confirmDeleteDebate: Are you sure you want to delete this debate? This cannot be undone.
debate.confirmDeleteReply: Are you sure you want to delete this reply?

colloquium.accessRequired: This colloquium requires 1 credit to read. Unlock to see the full debate and all philosopher arguments.
colloquium.proposeCost: 5 credits — AI selects 4 philosophers for your topic
colloquium.proposeHint: Describe a philosophical question or dilemma. AI will choose the best 4 philosophers to debate it.
colloquium.proposeContentPlaceholder: Provide context, examples, or specific aspects you want explored...
colloquium.creationTimeout: Colloquium created! Philosopher replies are being generated in the background.
colloquium.searchPhilosopher: Search by name, era, or school...
colloquium.allPhilosophersAdded: All available philosophers have already been added.
colloquium.visibilityOpenHint: Everyone can see and join this colloquium
colloquium.visibilityClosedHint: Only you (and invited users) can see this colloquium
colloquium.openDebateCost: 3 credits — Free to read, 1 credit to participate
colloquium.openDebateHint: Anyone can read for free. Add philosophers on demand. You decide when the verdict is generated.
colloquium.confirmDeleteColloquium: Are you sure you want to delete this colloquium? All replies, votes, and audio will be permanently removed.
